title: bismalva alcea
intro: Bismalva alcea, also known as Bismalva or Balsamroot, is a flowering plant native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and a rosette of green, lobed leaves. The root and rhizome of the plant contain mucilage, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The health benefits of Bismalva alcea include relief from digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. Bismalva alcea is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
